CREATE TABLE test_table_a (id INT, value VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE test_table_b (id INT, ref_id INT);
INSERT INTO test_table_a (id, value) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, NULL), (3, 'test3');
INSERT INTO test_table_b (id, ref_id) VALUES (1, 1), (2, 2), (3, NULL);
SELECT id, value FROM test_table_a WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table_b WHERE ref_id IS NULL;
SELECT (SELECT value FROM test_table_a WHERE id = 2) IS NULL AS is_null_check;
SELECT a.id, a.value FROM test_table_a a JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE b.ref_id IS NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_table_a WHERE NOT(value IS NULL);
SELECT id, CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'NULL' ELSE 'NOT NULL' END AS null_check FROM test_table_a;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table_a WHERE value IS NULL) AS null_count;
SELECT a.id FROM test_table_a a LEFT JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE b.ref_id IS NULL AND a.value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, NVL(b.ref_id, -1) AS ref_id_check FROM test_table_a a LEFT JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE a.value IS NULL;
SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table_a WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_table_a) AS subquery WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT b.id FROM test_table_b b WHERE b.ref_id IS NULL AND b.id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table_a WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
SELECT a.id, COALESCE(b.ref_id, 0) AS ref_id_info FROM test_table_a a FULL OUTER JOIN test_table_b b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE a.value IS NULL;
